skill.name: firebase-data-connect
skill.description: Build and deploy Firebase Data Connect backends with PostgreSQL. Use for schema design, GraphQL queries/mutations, authorization, and SDK generation for web, Android, iOS, and Flutter apps.
skill.summary: Build and deploy Firebase Data Connect backends with PostgreSQL. Use for schema design, GraphQL quer...
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: This is official Firebase documentation skill providing GraphQL schema examples and CLI commands. Static scanner flags are false positives: shell commands are documentation examples, path traversal is SDK output paths, and crypto mentions are algorithm name references. No actual code execution or malicious patterns present.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Patterns in Documentation
medium_findings.description: Documentation contains bash command examples like `npx firebase-tools` commands. These are documentation examples in markdown code blocks, not actual shell execution code. Pattern found across 486 locations in examples and reference files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are markdown documentation examples of Firebase CLI commands, not actual shell execution code. The backtick detection is a false positive from documentation syntax.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Sequences in SDK Config
low_findings.description: SDK output directories use `../` relative paths like `../web/src/lib/dataconnect`. This is legitimate SDK generation configuration, not a path traversal vulnerability.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are intentional relative paths for SDK output directories in Firebase connector configuration, not user-controlled path traversal.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded IP Address (Android Emulator)
low_findings.description: IP address `10.0.2.2` is the standard Android emulator loopback address pointing to host localhost. This is legitimate documentation for emulator setup.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: 10.0.2.2 is the documented Android emulator standard loopback - this is legitimate documentation, not a security issue.

low_findings.title: Cryptographic Algorithm Name References
low_findings.description: Documentation mentions algorithm names like MD5, SHA1 in context of algorithm identification, not actual weak crypto implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are algorithm name references in documentation context, not actual weak cryptographic implementations. The skill does not implement crypto.

low_findings.title: Environment File References
low_findings.description: Documentation references `.env` files for configuration. This is standard environment variable documentation, not credential exposure.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: References to .env files are standard configuration documentation, not actual credential exposure or exfiltration.

content.user_title: Build Firebase Data Connect backends with GraphQL and PostgreSQL
content.value_statement: Firebase Data Connect requires understanding GraphQL schema design, authentication patterns, and SDK integration across multiple platforms. This skill provides comprehensive templates, examples, and reference documentation to build production-ready backends.
actual_capabilities: Define GraphQL schemas with @table, @col, @ref directives for PostgreSQL tables
Create queries and mutations with filtering, ordering, pagination, and transactions
Implement row-level security with @auth, @check, and @redact directives
Generate type-safe SDKs for web (TypeScript), Android (Kotlin), iOS (Swift), and Flutter (Dart)
Configure Firebase CLI for local development, emulation, and production deployment
Implement vector search and full-text search capabilities
limitations: Does not execute Firebase CLI commands or deploy code - only provides documentation and examples
Cannot access Firebase project or database directly - skill is for learning and reference
Requires manual SDK generation step using Firebase CLI before client integration

use_cases.title: Build a user-owned data backend
use_cases.description: Create schemas where users own their data with row-level security. Implement upsert mutations for user profiles and protect data with authentication checks.

use_cases.title: Implement complex queries and relationships
use_cases.description: Define one-to-many and many-to-many relationships between entities. Access nested data through auto-generated nested field accessors.
use_cases.target_user: Full-stack developers working with relational data

use_cases.title: Generate cross-platform SDKs
use_cases.description: Generate type-safe SDKs for web, Android, iOS, and Flutter from a single GraphQL schema. Integrate with real-time subscriptions using Flow.
use_cases.target_user: Mobile and web developers building cross-platform apps

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic CRUD schema
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Data Connect schema for a [entity type] with name, description, and timestamps. Add public list and get queries. Add authenticated create, update, and delete mutations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - basic schema and operation setup

prompt_templates.title: Build user-owned resource pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Data Connect schema for [resource] where each item is owned by the authenticated user. Use @auth(level: USER) and auth.uid references. Add a query to list only the current user's items.

prompt_templates.title: Implement complex filtering with relationships
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a schema with [entity A] and [entity B] in a many-to-many relationship. Add a query that filters [entity A] by [criteria] and includes related [entity B] items with pagination.

prompt_templates.title: Set up transaction for multi-step operation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a transaction that atomically creates a [entity A] and multiple [entity B] items in a single operation. Use response binding to get generated IDs and include them in related records.

output_examples.input: Create a schema for a blog with posts and comments
output_examples.output: Schema defines Post and Comment tables with one-to-many relationship. Queries allow listing posts with comment counts. Mutations require authentication for creating comments.

output_examples.input: How do I set up vector search for similarity matching?
output_examples.output: Add @col(dataType: "vector", size: 1536) to an array field. Use the built-in vector similarity functions in queries with @auth(level: PUBLIC) for matching.

best_practices: Use @unique constraints on fields that should be unique per entity, like user email or composite keys for junction tables
Prefer @default(expr: "request.time") and @default(expr: "uuidV4()") over manual value generation for timestamps and IDs
Use @transaction for operations that create multiple related records to ensure atomicity and data consistency
anti_patterns: Avoid hardcoding authentication levels - always verify @auth directives match your security requirements
Do not skip @unique constraints on fields that must be unique - this causes data integrity issues at scale
Avoid deeply nested relationships without pagination - always add limit/offset to prevent large result sets

faq.question: What is Firebase Data Connect?
faq.answer: Firebase Data Connect is a PostgreSQL-based backend service that uses GraphQL for schema definition and operations. It auto-generates type-safe SDKs for client apps.

faq.question: How does authentication work?
faq.answer: Use @auth(level: USER) on operations. Access the authenticated user via auth.uid in expressions like @default(expr: "auth.uid").

faq.question: Can I use transactions?
faq.answer: Yes, wrap multiple mutations in a @transaction block. Use response binding to reference values from earlier operations in the same transaction.

faq.question: What SDKs are supported?
faq.answer: TypeScript/JavaScript for web, Kotlin for Android, Swift for iOS, and Dart for Flutter. Generate SDKs using firebase-tools dataconnect:sdk:generate.

faq.question: How do I test locally?
faq.answer: Run npx firebase-tools@latest emulators:start --only dataconnect to start the local emulator with seed data support.

faq.question: What is vector search?
faq.answer: Vector search uses @col(dataType: "vector") for AI-powered similarity matching. Useful for recommendation systems and semantic search.
